transcripts.plain-text-transcript.txt:
> What is memory?
Memory is persistence.

> Why does it matter?
It enables continuity.

> How do we build it?
With structured storage.
transcripts.claude-code-session.jsonl:
> How should we store memory?
Use structured local storage.
transcripts.codex-session.jsonl:
> What did we decide about auth?
We chose JWT tokens with refresh cookies.
transcripts.slack-export.json:
> We should migrate the auth flow.
Passkeys are a good candidate.

> Lets document the tradeoffs.
transcripts.chatgpt-conversation.json:
> Why did we switch to passkeys?
We wanted phishing-resistant login with better UX.

conversation_chunks:
> What is memory?
Memory is persistence.

> Why does it matter?
It enables continuity.

> How do we build it?
With structured storage.

auth.text: The authentication module uses JWT tokens for session management. Tokens expire after 24 hours. Refresh tokens are stored in HttpOnly cookies.

notes.text: Sprint planning: migrate auth to passkeys by Q3. Evaluate ChromaDB alternatives for vector search.

backend.text: The authentication module uses JWT tokens for session management. Tokens expire after 24 hours. Refresh tokens are stored in HttpOnly cookies.
Database migrations are handled by Alembic. We use PostgreSQL 15 with connection pooling via pgbouncer.
